title: pleiospermium littorale
intro: Pleiospermium littorale, also known as Chinese boxwood or boxwood, is a type of plant that belongs to the family Celastraceae. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The health benefits of Pleiospermium littorale include its potential to reduce symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its ability to improve digestion and reduce stress. Pleiospermium littorale can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
